findings.evidence: `rg -n "pointsMap_mapValue|mapValue_pointsMap|pointsMap.*mapValue|mapValue.*pointsMap" TauCeti` finds no declaration; the only available generic API is `TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/HopfMap.lean:119` `lemma mapValue_mapDomain`.
findings.fix: Add a specialized naturality theorem for `pointsMap`, probably a `[simp]` theorem derived from `TauCeti.AlgHom.mapValue_mapDomain`, with both sides stated in terms of `pointsMap` and `AlgHom.mapValue`.
findings.issue: `pointsMap` is public as the contravariant map on points, but there is no `pointsMap_mapValue`/`mapValue_pointsMap` lemma showing it commutes with postcomposition in the value algebra, so users cannot use this as a natural transformation without unfolding to `AlgHom.mapDomain`.

summary: The exposed `pointsMap` has the basic identity/composition and character-precomposition API, but it is missing the specialized naturality lemma needed to use it as a map of functors of points.
